skill.name: commit-message-generator
skill.description: Generate appropriate commit messages based on Git diffs
skill.summary: Generate appropriate commit messages based on Git diffs
skill.author: Daisuke897

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external Git commands and a high-severity weak cryptography pattern. The Git command findings are true positives but appear purpose-aligned and read-only, while the cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, or destructive Git execution instructions were found.

medium_findings.title: External Git Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run git status and git diff commands. These commands are legitimate for commit message generation and are read-only, but they execute local Git commands and may expose repository changes to the assistant context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly listed in SKILL.md and are necessary for the stated purpose. Risk is moderate because they are read-only and no network or destructive action is present.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Suggestion Requires Careful Quoting
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to suggest git add and git commit command strings. The surrounding instructions prohibit executing them, but generated shell commands should quote filenames and commit messages carefully before users copy them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is a suggestion rather than executable code, and SKILL.md lines 11 and 31 prohibit commits and pushes. The concern is limited to copy-and-paste command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The scanner reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at this line, but the line only contains the skill description. No cryptographic code or algorithm reference was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact reported location contains only the front matter description. No evidence of hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm use appears in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Git Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on git status and git diff command execution to inspect repository changes. This is expected for the skill, but it remains an external command pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command pattern is directly present in the skill instructions. The context supports legitimate read-only use rather than malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Generate Commit Messages from Git Diffs
content.value_statement: Writing clear commit messages from complex diffs takes time and often leads to vague history. This skill inspects Git changes and suggests Conventional Commits messages with focused change summaries.
actual_capabilities: Runs git status to identify modified files.
Reads unstaged or staged diffs with git diff commands.
Generates English commit messages using Conventional Commits format.
Suggests logical commit splits when changes cover different concerns.
Lists target files for each proposed commit group.
Provides optional bullet summaries after the commit header.
limitations: Does not execute git commit or git push.
Requires access to a Git repository with local changes.
Quality depends on the clarity and scope of the available diff.
Does not verify project-specific commit rules beyond Conventional Commits.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Clean Feature Commit
use_cases.description: Generate a concise Conventional Commits message after completing a focused feature change.

use_cases.title: Split Mixed Work Into Commits
use_cases.description: Review a broad diff and separate unrelated changes into logical commit suggestions.

use_cases.title: Improve Pull Request History
use_cases.description: Create clearer commit messages before opening or updating a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Commit Message
prompt_templates.scenario: You have one focused change and need a suggested message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the commit-message-generator skill to inspect my current Git diff and suggest one Conventional Commits message.

prompt_templates.title: Staged Changes Only
prompt_templates.scenario: You staged the files that belong in the next commit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze only my staged changes and suggest a Conventional Commits message with a short bullet summary.

prompt_templates.title: Split Commit Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Your working tree includes several unrelated changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect my Git changes and tell me whether they should be split into multiple commits. Include target files and messages for each group.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced History Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: You want precise commit groupings before preparing a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the full diff, identify logical commit boundaries, and propose Conventional Commits messages with rationale for each split.

output_examples.input: A diff adds validation to the sign-in form and updates related tests.
output_examples.output:
feat(auth): validate sign-in form inputs

- Adds required field checks for email and password
- Updates sign-in tests for validation states

output_examples.input: A diff includes a refactor of API helpers and a separate button style update.
output: style(ui): align button spacing with design tokens

output_examples.input: A staged diff fixes a null handling bug in export generation.
output_examples.output:
fix(exports): handle missing optional fields

- Prevents export failures when optional data is absent
- Adds coverage for empty field values

best_practices: Stage related files before asking for a commit message.
Review suggested commands before running them in a shell.
Keep each commit focused on one logical change.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to commit or push changes for you.
Do not combine unrelated features, fixes, and refactors in one commit.
Do not accept generated messages without checking project conventions.

faq.question: Does this skill create commits automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only suggests messages and commands. Users must run any Git commands themselves.

faq.question: What commit format does it use?
faq.answer: It follows Conventional Commits with a one-line header and optional bullet summary.

faq.question: Can it work with staged changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect staged changes with git diff --cached when needed.

faq.question: Can it suggest multiple commits?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend splitting changes when a diff covers multiple concerns.

faq.question: Does it support languages other than English?
faq.answer: The skill instructions require commit messages in English.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It reads local Git diffs, so users should only run it where sharing code context with the assistant is acceptable.
